Documentation for implementing message flashing in Flask applications, including examples and templates.

# Purpose
The content describes the implementation and usage of a message flashing system in a Flask web application. This system provides user feedback by allowing messages to be recorded at the end of a request and accessed during the next request. The documentation includes examples of how to implement simple flashing using Flask's `flash` function and how to display these messages in HTML templates using Jinja2. It also explains how to categorize flash messages to enhance user feedback, such as displaying error messages with a specific style. Additionally, the content covers filtering flash messages by category, which allows developers to render different categories in separate blocks within the user interface. This functionality is crucial for improving user interaction by providing timely and relevant feedback.
# Content Summary
The document describes the implementation and usage of the message flashing system in a Flask web application. The flashing system allows developers to provide feedback to users by recording a message at the end of a request and displaying it on the next request. This is typically used in conjunction with layout templates to display messages to users.

The document provides a simple example of how to implement message flashing in a Flask application. It includes a basic application setup with routes for the index and login pages. The login route demonstrates how to flash a message when a user successfully logs in. The example also includes HTML templates (`layout.html`, `index.html`, and `login.html`) that use Jinja2 templating to display flashed messages.

The document explains how to use categories with flashed messages, which was introduced in version 0.3 of Flask. By default, messages have a category of `'message'`, but developers can specify alternative categories, such as `'error'`, to provide more specific feedback. The document shows how to modify the template to handle messages with categories.

Additionally, the document covers filtering flash messages, a feature added in version 0.9. Developers can filter messages by category using the `get_flashed_messages` function, allowing them to render each category in a separate block. This is useful for organizing messages and providing a clear user interface.

Overall, the document provides detailed instructions on implementing and customizing the message flashing system in Flask applications, including examples of code and templates.
